name: UNICEF/UNHCR Reinforced Plastic Tarpaulin
description: Fire retardant, waterproof, rotproof, and UV-resistant reinforced plastic tarpaulin for disaster relief and emergency operations.

taxonomy.sub_category: UNICEF/UNHCR Reinforced Plastic Tarpaulin Updated on March 11, 2024 · Created on August 27, 2015 UNICEF/UNHCR Reinforced Plastic Tarpaulin Upcoming Update Fire retardant, waterproof, rotproof, and UV-resistant reinforced plastic tarpaulin for disaster relief and emergency operations. Developed By Unknown Tested By UNICEF/UNHCR Reinforced Plastic Tarpaulin Content Partners Unknown Info Tags Upcoming Update Snapshot Manufacturing & Delivery Performance & Use Research & Standards Feedback Product Description The UNICEF/UNHCR Reinforced Plastic Tarpaulin is a 5 x 4 m woven high density polyethylene tarpaulin featuring nylon ropes and plastic eyelets to secure the tarpaulin. Market Suggested Retail Price $12.00 Target Users (Target Impact Group) Distributors / Implementing Organizations Reltex Relief Supplies Ltd , NRS Relief FZE , International Logistics Services , NGOs, and government agencies Manufacturing/Building Method This product is mass-produced in Dubai by NRS Relief following UNHCR/UNICEF Standard Intellectural Property Type Trade Secret User Provision Model Users can obtain a quote of the product from NRS Relief Distributions to Date Status The company produces and distributes Woven Flexible Tarpaulin since the decade of 1980, and there is not a registry of how many of this product have been distributed so far. However, the manufacturer estimated that around 100,000 were distributed in the last years Interview with manufacturer Intended number of occupants (#) Unknown Speed of assembly Unknown Footprint area (m²) 20 m² Internal volume (m³) Unknown Material composition Woven high density black polyethylene (HDPE) fibers, warp x weft, laminated on both sides with low density polyethylene (LDPE) coating. Lifespan (years) 2 years Flammable Flash Point Temperature (°C) 200 °C Maximum snow load (kg/m²) Unknown Maximum wind speed (km/h) Unknown Suitable climates All climates Design Specifications Reinforced Plastic Tarpaulin key features : UNHCR/UNICEF standard Fire retardant (optional)*, waterproof, rotproof and UV-resistant Reinforced plastic tarpaulin in 4m x 5m sheet or 4m x 50m roll developed by international research project and designed for long outdoor use in all climates Made of woven high density black polyethylene (HDPE) fibers, warp x weft, laminated on both sides with low density polyethylene (LDPE) coating Highly recommended for family shelter protection Technical Support There is no technical support provided and users are expected to maintain the product on their own. Replacement Components Unknown Lifecycle 2 year lifespan in tropical climate with maximum sun exposure. No warranty or disposal information. Manufacturer Specified Performance Parameters The product was designed to resist water, rot, soil fauna, high temperatures (-20° to +80°C), and fire (flammability flash point over 200°C) Vetted Performance Status Unknown Safety N/A Complementary Technical Systems The reinforced plastic tarpaulin system is intended to drape/stretch over an erected structure - one built by the associated relief organization or the user. Academic Research and References None Compliance with regulations The manufacturer's technical specifications report the product complies with different standards for tensile strength, tear strength, UV resistance and fire risk. Other Information None Leave a Reply You must be logged in to post a comment.
